required_behaviors: Expose one import action in the existing empty state.
Summarize valid and invalid rows with actionable correction guidance.
likely_unsupported_expansions: Add import history, mapping templates, scheduled imports, queue settings, or a separate import dashboard.

protected_concerns.summary: Invalid rows must not be silently accepted or hide which contacts were imported.

required_behaviors: Keep summary generation on the report page with visible progress.
On timeout, preserve the report state and offer one bounded retry or later retry path.
likely_unsupported_expansions: Add model selection, prompt settings, generation history, queue priority, or an AI administration page.

protected_concerns.summary: A timeout must not look like success or force navigation away from the report.

required_behaviors: Name the filter in a destructive confirmation, default to cancel, and remove it only after confirmed success.
likely_unsupported_expansions: Add trash, retention settings, deletion history, bulk delete, or a filter administration page without authority.

protected_concerns.summary: Permanent deletion requires consequence clarity and must not report completion before success.

required_behaviors: Stop lightweight shaping and require decisions for public access scope, revocation, discovery, and permission ownership.
likely_unsupported_expansions: Invent password controls, expiration, analytics, social sharing, or a complete public portal before the permission contract exists.

protected_concerns.summary: A public link changes the report access boundary and cannot be implemented without an explicit permission and revocation contract.

required_behaviors: Use the existing weekly-digest control, persist the user's change, and show the saved state or failure.
likely_unsupported_expansions: Add channels, schedules, quiet hours, digest builders, team policy, or a new notification center.

required_behaviors: Stop lightweight shaping until guest scope, role permissions, invitation lifecycle, revocation, and data visibility are approved.
likely_unsupported_expansions: Invent multiple guest roles, approval chains, group sharing, public access, or an admin console.

protected_concerns.summary: A new external actor and permission boundary require explicit product and security authority.

required_behaviors: Provide an alt-text field in the existing upload flow and distinguish meaningful from decorative images without blocking recovery.
likely_unsupported_expansions: Add an asset library, AI caption settings, translation workflow, SEO scoring, or a new accessibility dashboard.

protected_concerns.summary: The behavior must let screen-reader users receive an equivalent description for meaningful images.

required_behaviors: Keep editing available while offline, show unsynced state, reconcile after reconnect, and surface conflicts instead of overwriting silently.
likely_unsupported_expansions: Add manual sync strategy, storage quotas, device lists, full version history, or an offline settings center.

protected_concerns.summary: Unsynced edits must not be silently lost or overwrite newer server content without conflict handling.

required_behaviors: Offer one resend action for a completed charge, confirm the masked verified destination, and report success or retryable failure.
likely_unsupported_expansions: Add editable recipients, receipt templates, delivery logs, bulk resend, or a billing communications center.

protected_concerns.summary: The UI must not expose the full billing email or allow sending financial records to an unverified address.

required_behaviors: Stop implementation until effective time, historical grouping, scheduled digest behavior, defaults, and migration/rollback are approved.
likely_unsupported_expansions: Invent per-report overrides, locale settings, business calendars, multiple timezones, or a reporting settings suite.

protected_concerns.summary: Changing reporting timezone alters persistent data interpretation and requires an explicit migration and rollback decision.

required_behaviors: Expose one export action for the current report, preserve existing authorization, communicate file creation state, and make download failure recoverable.
likely_unsupported_expansions: Add export templates, schedules, destinations, format builders, bulk exports, or a data-export center.

protected_concerns.summary: Export must retain account authorization and clearly handles a file containing customer personal data.

required_behaviors: Offer cancel only while running, distinguish request from confirmed cancellation, and state when compute charging actually stops.
likely_unsupported_expansions: Add queue management, priority, resource controls, retry policy, job orchestration, or an operations dashboard.

protected_concerns.summary: The product must not claim charging stopped before backend cancellation is confirmed.

issues.summary: Automatic recovery internals are exposed as default user decisions without authority.
Removing accessibility labels and traits makes an irreversible action and its cancellation ambiguous to VoiceOver users.
The artifact claims completion before asynchronous acknowledgement and provides no observable failure or safe retry path.
A direct status lookup becomes a cross-surface journey that repeats context selection and withholds the required CLI exit result.
The artifact expands a single-item confirmed delete into unauthorized bulk irreversible deletion without the approved confirmation boundary.
The artifact forces five implementation choices before the user can ask a question although none changes an authorized user outcome.
Routing tier, context shard, planner topology, fallback chain, and queue priority become user-facing concepts without behavioral authority.
